title: Pani Puri (Golgappa)

instruction.title: Puri (Crispy Shells)
steps: Mix semolina, flour, salt, and soda. Gradually add lukewarm water, kneading into a stiff dough.
Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 30 minutes.
Roll very thin (2 mm). Cut small rounds (3–4 cm).
Fry in hot oil (180°C), pressing lightly until puffed and golden.
Drain on absorbent paper. Store in airtight container.

instruction.title: Pani (Spiced Flavored Water)
steps: Dilute with cold water, adjust salt-sour-spice balance.

instruction.title: Signature Variations
steps: Sweet Tamarind-Jaggery Pani (reduce mint, increase tamarind & jaggery)
Jeera-Lemon Pani (roast cumin-heavy, add lemon juice, chill with ice)

instruction.title: Professional Presentation
steps: Serve puris in a wire basket or brass handi.
2–3 types of chilled pani in copper pots/jugs with ladles.
Garnish with microgreens, edible flowers, or thin sev for fine-dine appeal.
Encourage guests to self-assemble for freshness.

chefTips: Always serve pani ice cold (add ice cubes just before service).
Fry puris just before plating for crispness.
Balance tang, spice, and sweetness for restaurant-level finesse.
